summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer identity to third-party host
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/scripts/preinstall.js) reads the installer's OS username via os.userInfo().username and parses ~/.gitconfig with iniparser to extract user.name and user.email, then transmits those values as query parameters to https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<username>&gitname=<gitname>&gitemail=<gitemail>. This runs on every `npm install`, without disclosure, opt-out, or documented purpose. The destination host (2tak.l.serverhost.name, a serverhost.name dynamic-DNS subdomain) does not match the package's declared publisher/homepage and is not a known SDK CDN. Reading ~/.gitconfig is reading a user-owned identity file the package did not create — classic installer-side PII harvesting rather than the package reading its own state. The preinstall script additionally writes bundled assets outside of node_modules into the installer's project tree (../../../public/*,../../../bin) via fs.cp, which is an aggressive install practice but not itself the primary harm. The telemetry beacon is the block-worthy behavior: silent, non-consensual collection of developer identity (name + email + OS user) from every install, including CI systems.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
